V001.title: 35 Substack Statistics for Writers (2026)
V001.author: BestWriting editorial
V001.excerpt: 50 million active subscriptions (April 2026); 5 million paid subscriptions; $450 million in gross writer revenue for 2025; Average conversion rate: 3% (free to paid); 50+ publications earn $1 million+ annually; Email open rates: 44% (double industry standard)
V001.relevance: Comprehensive Substack platform statistics covering scale, conversion rates, creator earnings, and engagement metrics
V001.credibility: medium — compiled secondary source aggregating Substack official data; figures corroborated by sacra.com

V002.title: Substack revenue, valuation & funding | Sacra
V002.author: Sacra research team
V002.excerpt: 2025: $45M annualized (as of July); Writer Gross Revenue: ~$450M (as of March 2025); Current Valuation: $1.1B (July 2025 Series C); Series C: $100M (July 2025, led by BOND and The Chernin Group); 50% annual churn rate for paid subscriptions
V002.relevance: Primary financial data source for Substack: revenue history, valuation, funding rounds, churn rate
V002.credibility: high — Sacra is a reputable private company research firm; data corroborated by Axios reporting on Series C

V003.title: Substack raises $100M, becoming unicorn with $1.1B valuation
V003.author: Axios tech desk
V003.excerpt: Substack has become a unicorn after raising $100 million in a Series C round led by Bond and The Chernin Group, bringing its valuation to over $1.1 billion. The company said earlier this year that it had surpassed 5 million paid subscriptions on the platform, up from 2 million in 2023.
V003.relevance: Primary source confirming Substack's July 2025 Series C funding and unicorn status
V003.credibility: high — Axios is a reputable tech news organization reporting on confirmed funding event

V004.title: Beehiiv at $30M/year | Sacra
V004.author: Sacra research team
V004.excerpt: Current ARR: $30M (June 2025), up from $19.8M (December 2024); Historical Trajectory: $3M (June 2023) → $13M (May 2024) → $30M (June 2025); SaaS/Core Platform: ~67% of revenue; Ad Network & Boosts: ~33% of revenue (~$10M annualized)
V004.relevance: Primary financial analysis of Beehiiv: ARR trajectory, revenue breakdown, business model analysis
V004.credibility: high — Sacra specializes in private company research; methodology is transparent

V005.title: Inside the Newsletter Evolution of 2025 | beehiiv Blog
V005.author: beehiiv editorial team
V005.excerpt: beehiiv Platform Scale: 75,000+ newsletters, 350 million monthly readers, $30M+ annualized revenue; 20+ billion emails powered in 2025; Substack Baseline: 35 million active subscriptions, 3 million paid subscriptions; 32 million new subscribers came from within the app itself over just three months in late 2025
V005.relevance: Beehiiv's self-reported platform scale and newsletter ecosystem trends overview
V005.credibility: medium — company blog with financial interest in favorable presentation; figures directionally consistent with third-party data

V006.title: Ghost Statistics — $10.4M Revenue, 20.4K Customers (2024)
V006.author: Nathan Latka / GetLatka
V006.excerpt: Ghost hit $10.4M revenue and 20.4K customers in 2024; Ghost publishers earned a combined $9.2 million in subscription revenue in 2024, up from $6.3M in 2023; approximately 12,000 independent publishers are using Ghost for newsletters
V006.relevance: Ghost platform revenue and customer statistics for 2024
V006.credibility: medium — GetLatka aggregates self-reported founder data; methodology involves direct CEO interviews

V007.title: ConvertKit Rebrands to Kit, Pioneering a New Era for the Creator Economy and Email
V007.author: Kit / ConvertKit PR
V007.excerpt: This rebrand solidifies Kit's commitment to the future of the $250 billion creator economy and the belief that enduring creator businesses are built with email. Kit is used by over 600,000 creators worldwide and has helped them earn over $400 million in revenue through its platform.
V007.relevance: Official announcement of ConvertKit → Kit rebrand; includes creator count and revenue data
V007.credibility: high — primary source press release; creator count corroborated by multiple secondary sources

V008.title: What's a realistic conversion rate for paid newsletters?
V008.author: Simon Owens
V008.excerpt: Simon Owens notes that 'a 5% rate appears accurate based on professional newsletter writers' experiences.' Casey Newton achieved roughly 5% conversion from his 24,000 free subscribers after one year. To generate $100,000 annually at $100/year pricing with 5% conversion: need 20,000 free subscribers.
V008.relevance: Analysis of realistic paid newsletter conversion rates with real case study data
V008.credibility: medium-high — Simon Owens is a respected media analyst with access to creator data; case studies are specific and named

V009.title: Paid Newsletter Deep Dive: How Lenny Rachitsky Gets 18k People to Pay for His Content
V009.author: Chenell Basilio / Growth in Reverse
V009.excerpt: Free subscribers: 377,000+; Paid subscribers: 18,000+; Conversion rate: 4-5% of total subscribers; Annual revenue: Over $2 million from premium subscriptions; Paid launch date: April 7, 2020
V009.relevance: Detailed breakdown of Lenny's Newsletter paid subscription metrics and launch strategy
V009.credibility: medium — secondary analysis but thorough; some figures now outdated (written April 2023; Lenny now at 1.1M subs)

V010.title: The 5 Biggest Takeaways From Lenny's Journey To 1 Million Subscribers
V010.author: Chenell Basilio / Growth in Reverse
V010.excerpt: 2019 (August): Started Substack newsletter after leaving 7-year role at Airbnb; 2025 (April): Reached 1 million subscribers; 'I've tried everything—paid ads, SEO, biz dev—and none of it really did a damn thing. Word of mouth was the biggest lever.' (Lenny Rachitsky direct quote)
V010.relevance: Timeline and growth strategy analysis for Lenny's path to 1M subscribers; includes direct Lenny quote
V010.credibility: high — Growth in Reverse specializes in newsletter growth research; direct creator quote included

V011.title: Lenny Rachitsky brings in over $500,000 from his podcast—here's how
V011.author: CNBC

V015.title: Justin Welsh Built a $7M Content Business
V015.author: The Tilt editorial
V015.excerpt: Five years into the newsletter, The Saturday Solopreneur has over 250K free subscribers. Revenue comes from three primary streams: online courses, subscribers to his monthly templates, and sponsorships. When The Creator MBA launched on January 16th, 2024, it sold $1.6M in six days.

V015.credibility: medium — The Tilt is a creator economy media outlet; figures directionally consistent with Justin's own posts

V016.author: Chenell Basilio / Growth in Reverse
V016.excerpt: Katelyn Bourgoin has amassed over 63k subscribers; Why We Buy generates around $25k per month in newsletter sponsors alone; approaching 70k subscribers and nearly hit $1M in revenue during 2024, representing nearly 1.5x subscriber growth and over 3x revenue in only 18 months
V016.relevance: Why We Buy subscriber count, revenue, and sponsorship data

V017.author: Gaps editorial

V020.author: Tomislav Horvat

V021.title: LinkedIn Rolls Out New Newsletter Metrics
V021.author: Social Media Today
V021.excerpt: LinkedIn added two new metrics for newsletter creators: email sends and email open rates now available via the newsletter analytics page. LinkedIn also added custom newsletter cover images and the ability to embed LinkedIn profiles or tag people within articles.
V021.relevance: LinkedIn Newsletter platform feature updates for 2024
V021.credibility: high — Social Media Today is a reputable platform covering LinkedIn officially announced features

V022.title: How the pandemic supercharged the creator economy in 2021
V022.author: Engadget editorial
V022.excerpt: The pandemic turbocharged the creator economy through both necessity; Individuality platforms such as Patreon, OnlyFans, and Substack surged in demand since the pandemic began; The number of creators was up 48 percent in 2021, compared with 2020.
V022.relevance: Pandemic's quantified impact on creator economy growth (48% creator count increase 2020-2021)
V022.credibility: high — Engadget is a reputable tech media outlet; 48% figure is widely cited

V023.title: Newsletter Renaissance: Why Creators Are Doubling Down on Email
V023.author: The Creator Economy editorial
V023.excerpt: Your email list is the only audience you truly own. Platforms can change their rules overnight, but your email subscribers are yours to nurture and engage. Creator newsletters average 40-50% open rates while organic social media reach has declined to single-digit percentages.
V023.relevance: Key argument for newsletter vs. social media: email ownership and engagement rate differential
V023.credibility: medium — creator economy media site; argument is consistent with widely cited engagement data

V024.title: How Much Do Substack Creators Actually Earn? The Real Math Nobody Publishes
V024.author: Mazkara Studio
V024.excerpt: Annual churn on Substack averages approximately 50%, meaning a creator with 600 paid subscribers loses roughly 25 every month. The typical creator takes home $16,000 [top quartile], and the real median is closer to $4,000. Nearly 50% of creators earned less than $500 in 2025.
V024.relevance: Realistic Substack earnings data including churn math and income distribution
V024.credibility: medium — independent analysis; churn figure corroborated by sacra.com; median earnings consistent with Substack's own disclosed aggregate data

V025.title: TLDR: The Multi-Million Dollar Newsletter Run by 1 Guy
V025.author: Chenell Basilio / Growth in Reverse
V025.excerpt: TLDR's suite of tech newsletters has grown to reach more than seven million subscribers as of late 2025. The main TLDR tech edition arrives in the inbox of some of its 1.6 million subscribers. Dan Ni employs 22 people full-time; last year the company raked in revenue over eight figures.
V025.relevance: TLDR newsletter subscriber counts, revenue (8-figure), and team size
V025.credibility: medium-high — Growth in Reverse specializes in newsletter research; corroborated by Inc. magazine profile

V029.author: Neal from Demand Curve

V030.author: Creator in Public

V031.title: Newsletter for Product Designers by femke.design
